data.content:
### Sandbox Creation Example (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v2.0.1/sandbox/page.mdx

An example demonstrating how to create a new sandbox instance using the `Sandbox.create` method. Replace '<template-name-or-id>' with the actual template name or ID.

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')

```

--------------------------------

### Run TypeScript code with Axios in E2B

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/code-interpreting/supported-languages/javascript/page.mdx

This snippet demonstrates how to run TypeScript code that utilizes the `axios` library to fetch data from an API. It shows the process of creating a sandbox, installing external packages like `axios` using `npm install`, and then executing the TypeScript code. The example includes handling of top-level await and ESM-style imports.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from "@e2b/code-interpreter";

// Create a new sandbox
const sbx = await Sandbox.create();

// Install the axios package
await sbx.commands.run("npm install axios");

// Run the code
const execution = await sbx.runCode(`
  import axios from "axios";

  const url: string = "https://api.github.com/status";
  const response = await axios.get(url);
  response.data;
`,
  { language: "ts" }
);

console.log(execution);
```

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

# Create a new sandbox
sbx = Sandbox.create()

# Install the axios package
sbx.commands.run("npm install axios")

#  Run the code
execution = sbx.run_code("""
  import axios from "axios";

  const url: string = "https://api.github.com/status";
  const response = await axios.get(url);
  response.data;
""",
    language="ts",
)

print(execution)
```

--------------------------------

### Execute Command in Sandbox (TypeScript Example)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/sandbox/page.mdx

This example demonstrates how to execute a command within the sandbox environment using the `commands.exec` method. It's shown in the context of starting an HTTP server, but can be used for any shell command. The output or any errors are typically handled by the command execution mechanism.

```typescript
// Start an HTTP server
await sandbox.commands.exec('python3 -m http.server 3000')
```

--------------------------------

### File and URL Opening in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.9.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Introduces a TypeScript method for opening files or URLs using the default application within the sandbox environment. The `open` function takes a string argument representing the file path or URL. This operation is asynchronous and returns a Promise.

```typescript
/**
 * Open a file or a URL in the default application.
 * @param fileOrUrl The file or URL to open.
 * @returns A Promise that resolves when the file or URL is opened.
 */
open(fileOrUrl: string): Promise<void>;

```

--------------------------------

### Screenshot Functionality in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.9.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Details the TypeScript method for capturing a screenshot within the sandbox environment. The `screenshot` function returns a Promise that resolves with a `Uint8Array` representing the image data (ArrayBufferLike).

```typescript
/**
 * Take a screenshot.
 * @returns A Promise that resolves with a Uint8Array representing the screenshot image data.
 */
screenshot(): Promise<Uint8Array<ArrayBufferLike>>;

```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox from Specified Template - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.4.0/sandbox/page.mdx

This overload of the static `create` method allows for the instantiation of a new sandbox from a specific sandbox template, identified by its name or ID. It accepts the template identifier and optional connection options (`SandboxOpts`), returning a Promise that resolves to the created sandbox instance. An example illustrates how to use it with a template name.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, template: string, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>

/**
 * Create a new sandbox from the specified sandbox template.
 *
 * @template S The type of the Sandbox class.
 * @param this The Sandbox class.
 * @param template The sandbox template name or ID.
 * @param opts Optional connection options.
 * @returns A Promise resolving to the sandbox instance for the new sandbox.
 */

// Example:
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')
```

--------------------------------

### Define SandboxError Base Class in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.4/errors/page.mdx

This TypeScript code defines the base SandboxError class, serving as the parent for all sandbox-related exceptions. It captures general sandbox errors. The constructor accepts a message argument and it is extended by several other error types.

```typescript
class SandboxError extends Error {
  constructor(message: any) {
    super(message);
  }
}

// Defined in errors.ts:14
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: SandboxError Constructor

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.5/errors/page.mdx

The base class for all errors originating from the sandbox environment. It accepts a generic message.

```typescript
new SandboxError(message: any): SandboxError
```

--------------------------------

### Keyboard Input Functions in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.9.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Provides a TypeScript method for simulating keyboard input within the sandbox. The `press` function allows for pressing single keys or sequences of keys, such as 'enter', 'space', or 'backspace'. This function is asynchronous and returns a Promise.

```typescript
/**
 * Press a key.
 * @param key The key to press (e.g., 'enter', 'space', 'backspace'). Can be a single key or an array of keys.
 * @returns A Promise that resolves when the key press is complete.
 */
press(key: string | string[]): Promise<void>;

```

--------------------------------

### Start E2B Sandbox and Execute Code (TypeScript and Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/page.mdx

Demonstrates how to create a sandbox, execute a simple Python command, and list files within the sandbox using both TypeScript and Python SDKs. It requires environment variables to be set.

```js
import 'dotenv/config'
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = await Sandbox.create() // By default the sandbox is alive for 5 minutes
const execution = await sbx.runCode('print("hello world")') // Execute Python inside the sandbox
console.log(execution.logs)

const files = await sbx.files.list('/')
console.log(files)
```

```python
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sbx = Sandbox.create() # By default the sandbox is alive for 5 minutes
execution = sbx.run_code("print('hello world')") # Execute Python inside the sandbox
print(execution.logs)

files = sbx.files.list("/")
print(files)
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox from Default Template - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.4.0/sandbox/page.mdx

The static `create` method, when called without a template argument, instantiates a new sandbox using the default `desktop` template. It is a generic method that takes optional connection options (`SandboxOpts`) and returns a Promise resolving to the created sandbox instance. An example demonstrates its usage.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>

/**
 * Create a new sandbox from the default `desktop` sandbox template.
 *
 * @template S The type of the Sandbox class.
 * @param this The Sandbox class.
 * @param opts Optional connection options.
 * @returns A Promise resolving to the sandbox instance for the new sandbox.
 */

// Example:
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
```

### Define RateLimitError Class in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.4/errors/page.mdx

This TypeScript code defines the RateLimitError class, which inherits from SandboxError. It is thrown when an API's rate limits have been exceeded, preventing further requests. The constructor accepts a message argument.

```typescript
class RateLimitError extends SandboxError {
  constructor(message: any) {
    super(message);
  }
}

// Defined in errors.ts:92
```

--------------------------------

### Mouse Control Functions in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.9.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Provides TypeScript methods for controlling the mouse within the sandbox environment. This includes performing double clicks, left clicks, middle clicks, right clicks, pressing and releasing mouse buttons, and moving the cursor to specific coordinates. These functions are asynchronous and return Promises.

```typescript
/**
 * Double left click on the mouse position.
 * @param x The x-coordinate.
 * @param y The y-coordinate.
 * @returns A Promise that resolves when the action is complete.
 */
doubleClick(x?: number, y?: number): Promise<void>;

/**
 * Drag the mouse from the given position to the given position.
 * @param from The starting position [x, y].
 * @param to The ending position [x, y].
 * @returns A Promise that resolves when the action is complete.
 */
drag(from: [number, number], to: [number, number]): Promise<void>;

/**
 * Left click on the mouse position.
 * @param x The x-coordinate.
 * @param y The y-coordinate.
 * @returns A Promise that resolves when the action is complete.
 */
leftClick(x?: number, y?: number): Promise<void>;

/**
 * Middle click on the mouse position.
 * @param x The x-coordinate.
 * @param y The y-coordinate.
 * @returns A Promise that resolves when the action is complete.
 */
middleClick(x?: number, y?: number): Promise<void>;

/**
 * Press the mouse button.
 * @param button The mouse button to press ('left', 'right', or 'middle'). Defaults to 'left'.
 * @returns A Promise that resolves when the action is complete.
 */
mousePress(button: "left" | "right" | "middle"): Promise<void>;

/**
 * Release the mouse button.
 * @param button The mouse button to release ('left', 'right', or 'middle'). Defaults to 'left'.
 * @returns A Promise that resolves when the action is complete.
 */
mouseRelease(button: "left" | "right" | "middle"): Promise<void>;

/**
 * Move the mouse to the given coordinates.
 * @param x The x-coordinate.
 * @param y The y-coordinate.
 * @returns A Promise that resolves when the action is complete.
 */
moveMouse(x: number, y: number): Promise<void>;

/**
 * Right click on the mouse position.
 * @param x The x-coordinate.
 * @param y The y-coordinate.
 * @returns A Promise that resolves when the action is complete.
 */
rightClick(x?: number, y?: number): Promise<void>;

```

--------------------------------

### Define SandboxError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.5/errors/page.mdx

Defines the base SandboxError class for all sandbox-related errors. It accepts an optional message and stack trace.

```TypeScript
class SandboxError extends Error {
  constructor(message?: string, stackTrace?: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "SandboxError";
    if (stackTrace) {
      this.stack = stackTrace;
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: TemplateError Constructor

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.5/errors/page.mdx

Indicates an error when a template uses an incompatible, older version of envd. It requires a string message.

```typescript
new TemplateError(message: string): TemplateError
```

### TypeScript: InvalidArgumentError Constructor

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.5/errors/page.mdx

Represents an error thrown when an invalid argument is provided to a function or method. It requires a string message.

```typescript
new InvalidArgumentError(message: string): InvalidArgumentError
```

### Define SandboxError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.6.0/errors/page.mdx

The base class for all errors originating from the sandbox environment. It serves as a common ancestor for more specific sandbox-related exceptions.

```typescript
class SandboxError extends Error {
  constructor(message: any) {
    super(message);
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Set Sandbox Timeout (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.4/sandbox/page.mdx

This example demonstrates how to set or adjust the timeout for a sandbox instance. After the specified timeout expires, the sandbox will be automatically terminated. The `setTimeout` method accepts the timeout duration in milliseconds and optional connection options.

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
// Set sandbox to automatically kill after 1 hour
await sandbox.setTimeout(3600000)
```

--------------------------------

### Execute Code in E2B Sandbox (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/packages/js-sdk/README.md

Demonstrates how to create an E2B sandbox instance, run a simple code snippet ('x = 1'), and then execute another snippet ('x+=1; x') to retrieve and log the result. This requires the E2B API key to be set as an environment variable.

```typescript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = await Sandbox.create()
await sbx.runCode('x = 1')

const execution = await sbx.runCode('x+=1; x')
console.log(execution.text)  // outputs 2
```

--------------------------------

type LineChart = PointChart & object;

type LineChartTypeDeclaration = {

Defines the TimeoutError class, representing various timeout scenarios, including sandbox timeouts and request timeouts. It accepts a string message.
